lastReleaseMessage: Triage and fix the three long-standing `apps/api` test fa...

barRaise.topFinding: The standing HIGH that rode eleven reports is closed: after nine days frozen, three releases shipped 2026-06-22 (v0.4.2 SPA build restore, v0.5.0 M7.1 multi-tenant polish, v0.5.1 test green-up to 116/116). M7.1 is verified against code by the 06-23 canonical audit and an independent read of the tenant-isolation surface: scope resolution is centralized in apps/api/src/lib/scope.ts, the X-Org-Slug header is advisory and re-validated server-side per request, and cross-org reads return 404. The lone carried defect is small and self-inflicted by the same ship: v0.5.0 added *.bak and scripts/.discord_last_posted.txt to .gitignore but never git rm --cached the copies already tracked in HEAD, so the ignore is a no-op and the tree is now dirty after every release. One git rm --cached of three files plus the pending DESIGN edits clears it.

actions.title: Untrack the gitignored-but-tracked files and finish the rebrand in one commit: git rm --cached scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak scripts/release.ps1.bak scripts/.discord_last_posted.txt, rename package.json:5 to 'Yes& Scheduler' with plain ASCII, and commit alongside the two pending docs/DESIGN.md drift-fix edits. Stops the perpetual post-release dirty tree and restores the clean-tree dashboard signal.
Reconcile .project-context.json scheduled_tasks to the real cadence; twice-weekly bar-raise is defensible now the app is feature-complete and parked.
Run pnpm install then pnpm --filter api test locally to confirm 116/116 green outside CI (vitest symlink does not survive the FUSE mount).
